content.question: What proportion of the documented 40% increase in SNI identification over 20 years, and specifically the threefold autism rise in the past decade, is attributable to improved diagnostic methods versus true prevalence change versus policy-induced artifacts (e.g., the 2020 deferral-rule change)?
content.reduction_path: Commission an independent epidemiological time-series analysis decomposing the trend by separating administrative data discontinuities (policy changes), diagnostic-tool adoption timelines, and age-specific identification rates; cross-validate with international diagnostic prevalence studies to benchmark whether Hungarian ASD and broader SEN rates align with expected clinical prevalence in comparable populations.
